raw_consumer_statement:
I made an outdoor furniture purchase of {$920.00} on my Macys card. They came to deliver it on XX/XX/year>. I refused the merchandise as several items were damaged and/or no longer wanted. I didn't even sign for anything at time of delivery attempt. I asked for documentation showing I refused the product and driver said they have no way of giving me that. It would be refunded automatically once it went back to the warehouse. 

A month later it still did not show credited back. I called customer service in which they said they needed to investigate and would call me back. They never did in which I then was left with no choice but to open a dispute case id XXXX XXXXXXXX. I received a letter from Macys dated XX/XX/XXXX stating that the charges were valid with a copy of the sales check. I never said I didnt make the purchase, but the fact of the dispute was that it was refused on delivery. I never took possession of it for the reasons outlined below. I called again on XX/XX/XXXX in order to try and resolve but was unsuccessful. I am owed a refund on my account in the amount of {$920.00} as I never took delivery of the outdoor patio furniture. This has been extremely frustrating, and I have spent hours on the phone trying to get this resolved. I was told numerous times " rest assured our back office will be handling this ''.
